our_argument_summary: Cycle 1 verdict 'Misleading' is well-supported. The dome predicts 'global geomagnetic field' decay but confirms with 'SAA region alone' data — a regional-to-global bait-and-switch. The 28 nT/yr threshold is a model input parameter (not derived), hardcoded into monitoring, and guaranteed to be met by the fastest-declining region on Earth. PROS-007 was registered post-hoc after public data was available (6-day turnaround). Cycle 2 revalidation confirms all points; minor citations strengthen the argument but verdict itself is solid.

raw-text/02-wins.txt — Confirmation text: 'SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025' (word 'alone' tacitly admitting global may not meet threshold)
raw-text/05-model.txt — 'Field decay rate: >=28 nT/year' listed as core model parameter (input, not output)
raw-text/05-model.txt — 'Field Decay Rate: >=28 nT/year (2000-2025), projected 2030: -32 nT/year' — no derivation from dome geometry
WIN-004 curmudgeon review (Cycle 1) — documents monitor.py Domain 8 monitors Tsumeb (TTB) station only, not global field
WIN-005 curmudgeon review (Cycle 1) — confirms Domain 8 is single-station monitoring
WIN-035 curmudgeon review (Cycle 1) — confirms Domain 8 checks SAA decay, not global average
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-037 maps to PROS-007. Domain 8 ('SAA Decay Rate TTB') monitors Tsumeb INTERMAGNET station against hardcoded >=28 nT/yr threshold. The station is located in the SAA core, Earth's fastest-declining magnetic region. No code path computes or tests a global field average. The dome's W011 entry (inject_ai_layer.py) stores WIN-037 as static confirmed. This is a single-point test masquerading as a global test.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded threshold check. Domain 8 loads live Tsumeb decay data but compares against static 28 nT/yr threshold. No dynamic computation of global dipole moment or global average surface field intensity. The confirmation is stored as static metadata (W011: 'confirmed' with static '~30 nT' observation). This is consistent with Cycle 1 findings.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET Tsumeb (TTB) station — live monitoring data
NOAA WMM2025 — claimed in confirmation text (released late 2024, available pre-registration)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The dome claims 'global' field decay but provides SAA-region confirmation only. Built-in ambiguity: if any region shows >=28 nT/yr, the prediction can claim credit as a 'global' confirmation. The fastest-declining region on Earth (SAA) will almost always meet the threshold, making this untestable globally. The model cannot fail this test as long as SAA continues its current decline trajectory.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None identified in Cycle 2. Cycle 1 noted monitor.py source not directly available, but cross-references from WIN-004/005/035 reviews confirm Domain 8 architecture. All points hold on re-examination.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain 8 ('SAA Decay Rate TTB') monitors Tsumeb station decay against hardcoded >=28 nT/yr threshold. Live INTERMAGNET data fetched but compared to static threshold with no global field computation. W011 entry in inject_ai_layer.py stored as static 'confirmed' metadata. Tagging 'hardcoded' because the test itself is a simple hardcoded threshold comparison with no adaptive or computed element.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Geomagnetic field decay is continuously monitored by NOAA WMM, ESA Swarm, IAGA IGRF, INTERMAGNET for decades. Global dipole moment decline ~5% per century is standard. SAA regional decay 20-35 nT/yr is tracked by INTERMAGNET and WMM technical reports. The dome relabels cause as 'aetheric field decay' instead of 'core convection and reversed-flux patches' but predicts no different numerical value. This is relabeling standard phenomenon with non-standard terminology.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: PROS-007 registered 2026-03-06, confirmed by 2026-03-12 (6-day turnaround). Confirmation cites 'NOAA WMM2025 / INTERMAGNET confirms SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025.' WMM2025 released late 2024, INTERMAGNET data continuously available. Both sources were public months before registration. Batch pattern: WIN-035 through WIN-039 all registered 2026-03-06, suggesting systematic review of already-available public data. SAA has declined at 20-35 nT/yr for years — predicting >=28 nT over 12 months guarantees success.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 28 nT/yr value appears as model parameter (raw-text/05-model.txt: 'Field decay rate: >=28 nT/year') but has no derivation from dome geometry. No formula connects disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome property to 28 nT/yr. The value matches observed SAA core decay from INTERMAGNET — it was adopted from observations, not derived from dome physics. Same issue as other parameter-as-prediction WINs.

holes_found.description: Cycle 1 identified: '~15 nT/yr globally' is imprecisely sourced. Global dipole moment decline (~5% per century) translates to surface field intensity decline that varies enormously by location. There is no universally agreed 'global nT/yr' number. The dome could argue: 'There IS no single global average — the SAA is the most meaningful region to monitor.' This is our weakest citation.
holes_found.recommendation: REVALIDATION NOTE (Cycle 2): This remains valid. However, the argument doesn't depend on pinning down the exact global rate. The core argument is the regional-global conflation visible in the dome's own text: 'global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT' vs 'SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT.' The word 'alone' is admission that global may not meet threshold. Shift argument weight away from '~15 nT/yr globally' and toward direct quoting of the conflation. This is factually stronger.

holes_found.description: Cycle 1 identified: Dome's own confirmation text ('SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT') is not quoted in our detail_evidence section. This is the strongest single piece of evidence — the dome's own words admitting the regional-vs-global distinction. Currently our text references it loosely but doesn't quote it directly.
holes_found.recommendation: REVALIDATION NOTE (Cycle 2): CONFIRM CRITICAL WEAKNESS. Add direct quote to detail_evidence. Suggest: 'The dome's own confirmation text reveals the bait-and-switch: it predicts "global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT" (PROS-007) but confirms with "SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025" — the word "alone" tacitly admitting the global field may not have met the threshold.' This single quote is more powerful than 3 paragraphs of inference.

holes_found.description: Cycle 1 identified: The 6-day registration-to-confirmation gap (2026-03-06 to 2026-03-12) is not mentioned in our text. This is strong evidence of post-hoc pattern. Combined with batch registration date (WIN-035 through WIN-039 all 2026-03-06), it indicates systematic data mining, not prospective prediction.
holes_found.recommendation: REVALIDATION NOTE (Cycle 2): CONFIRM. Add to detail_evidence: 'PROS-007 was registered 6 March 2026 and confirmed by 12 March 2026 — a 6-day turnaround using WMM2025 data publicly available since December 2024. This pattern recurs across the batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date, suggesting systematic data mining from already-published sources rather than prospective prediction.' Current text mentions it but should be prominent.

holes_found.description: Cycle 1 identified: 28 nT/yr is listed as a core model PARAMETER, making the 'prediction' circular — measuring a value you built into your model is not prediction. Current text mentions this but doesn't stress it enough.
holes_found.recommendation: REVALIDATION NOTE (Cycle 2): CONFIRM. Strengthen the emphasis: 'The dome lists "Field decay rate: >=28 nT/year" as a core model parameter (raw-text/05-model.txt, line 42). Parameters are inputs, not outputs. Measuring that an observation matches a parameter you adopted from the same observation is circular — not predictive. This is measurement of your own input, confirmed by data you built the threshold around.'

holes_found.recommendation: REVALIDATION NOTE (Cycle 2): The detail_extra already contains appropriate cross-reference. When WIN-042 is reviewed in Cycle 2, cross-validate whether these are genuinely independent predictions or same observation counted twice.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own confirmation text is the most devastating piece of evidence. PROS-007 predicts 'global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT.' The confirmation says 'SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT.' The word 'alone' is a tacit admission that the global field may not have met the threshold — the regional measurement was substituted because it passed. This bait-and-switch should be quoted verbatim and highlighted as the core 'Misleading' verdict evidence.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt line 44: 'The global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT in the 12 months since March 2025'
raw-text/02-wins.txt line 45: 'SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025' (emphasis on 'alone')

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own monitoring infrastructure (monitor.py Domain 8) only checks a single INTERMAGNET station (Tsumeb, TTB) in the SAA core. It does not compute or test any global field average. This architectural fact proves the claim was never designed to be testable globally — only locally in the fastest-declining region. The code itself confirms the regional-global conflation.
sources: WIN-004 curmudgeon review: Domain 8 monitors TTB station only
WIN-005 curmudgeon review: no global field computation
WIN-035 curmudgeon review: Domain 8 tests single SAA decay, not global average

stronger_arguments.description: 28 nT/yr is adopted as a core model PARAMETER, not derived from dome geometry. The model was configured to match the observation, then predicts it will match — this is circular. The dome does not show how disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or any dome property implies >=28 nT/yr decay. The value is observationally adopted, making the 'prediction' a tautology.
sources: raw-text/05-model.txt: no derivation of this value from dome geometry

stronger_arguments.description: The SAA core has been declining at 25-35 nT/yr for over two decades (CHAOS-7, INTERMAGNET records). Predicting >=28 nT decline over 12 months guarantees confirmation — it's like predicting rain in Seattle in winter. The prediction has near-zero information content. A meaningful prediction would forecast the rate of change of decay (acceleration/deceleration) or predict when the SAA will stabilize — not whether it will continue the trend of the last 20 years.
sources: CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic model: SAA core intensity 2000-2025 shows consistent ~28-30 nT/yr decline
INTERMAGNET Tsumeb data: multiple decades of consistent regional decay

stronger_arguments.description: The 6-day registration-to-confirmation gap (2026-03-06 to 2026-03-12) combined with batch registration of five related predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) indicates systematic data mining from already-published sources rather than prospective prediction. WMM2025 was released in late 2024; INTERMAGNET data is continuously available. The 'prospective' claim is undermined by this temporal pattern.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt: batch of five predictions all registered 2026-03-06
WMM release timeline: WMM2025 public late 2024

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model correctly identifies that the geomagnetic field is weakening and highlights the South Atlantic Anomaly as Earth's most dramatic current manifestation. The SAA is indeed a region of intense scientific interest — it affects satellite radiation hardening, polar flight doses, and represents the most significant geomagnetic feature of the current epoch. Monitoring that the SAA has declined ~30 nT in a single year (2025) is factually accurate for that region.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a well-documented phenomenon is not predicting it. Global dipole moment decline (~5% per century) and SAA regional decay (20-35 nT/yr) have been continuously monitored by NOAA WMM, IAGA IGRF, ESA Swarm, and INTERMAGNET for decades. The dome adds no unique prediction: no mechanism explaining why the rate is 28-30 nT/yr (not 20, not 35), no forecast of when decline will reverse, and no derivation from dome geometry. Critically, PROS-007 predicted 'global' decay but confirmed with 'SAA region alone' data — a goalpost shift that converts the test from global (likely to fail) to regional (guaranteed to pass). The threshold was set within the established trend, eliminating discriminatory power.

deeper_analysis:
CYCLE 2 REVALIDATION FINDINGS:

**Verdict Status: HOLDS with high confidence (0.92, up from Cycle 1 0.88)**

The 'Misleading' verdict is correct and defensible. Cycle 2 revalidation confirms all Cycle 1 major findings:

1. **Regional-Global Conflation (STRONGEST)**: The dome predicts 'global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT' (PROS-007) but confirms with 'SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT' (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 45). The word 'alone' tacitly admits the global field may not meet the threshold. This is the core 'Misleading' evidence and should be quoted directly in our text.

2. **Circular Parameter-as-Prediction**: 28 nT/yr is listed as a core model parameter (raw-text/05-model.txt). Parameters are inputs. Predicting that your model's input parameter will be confirmed by data you used to set it is tautological. The dome provides no derivation connecting dome geometry to this specific decay rate.

3. **Hardcoded Monitoring of Single Region**: monitor.py Domain 8 monitors Tsumeb (TTB) station only — the fastest-declining location on Earth. No global field average is computed. The model cannot fail this test globally if it passes locally. This architectural fact proves the claim was never designed to be testable globally.

4. **Trivial Threshold with Guaranteed Confirmation**: SAA core has declined 25-35 nT/yr for two decades. Setting threshold at >=28 nT for 12 months is like predicting rain in Seattle in winter. The prediction has near-zero information content — any model that reads the SAA trend data would 'predict' the same thing. This fails the basic test of a scientific prediction: it must be discriminating (i.e., could actually fail).

5. **Post-Hoc Batch Pattern**: PROS-007 registered 2026-03-06, confirmed 2026-03-12 (6-day turnaround). WIN-035 through WIN-039 all registered same date. WMM2025 public late 2024. INTERMAGNET data continuously available. Temporal pattern indicates data mining, not prospective prediction.

**Confidence Increase (0.88 → 0.92) Rationale**:
- Cycle 1 Concern: '~15 nT/yr globally' is imprecisely sourced. Cycle 2 Revalidation: This can be addressed by shifting argument weight to direct quotation of dome's own 'SAA region alone' text (which doesn't require a specific global nT/yr number). The verdict doesn't depend on this figure.
- Cycle 1 Concern: 6-day registration gap 'not mentioned enough' in current text. Cycle 2: It IS mentioned in detail_evidence but could be more prominent. For Cycle 2, we can recommend a major_rewrite to elevate it.
- Cycle 1 Concern: Code_analysis tags needed validation. Cycle 2: Revalidated against WIN-004/005/035 reviews. All tags (hardcoded, relabels_standard, post_hoc, derives_from_dome) hold.

**Text Quality Assessment**:
- Current detail_evidence (1876 chars) is substantive but has structural issues:
  - The dome's own 'SAA region alone' quote is referenced but not quoted verbatim.
  - The 6-day registration gap is mentioned but buried.
  - The parameter-as-input circularity is present but could be more prominent.
  - The '~15 nT/yr globally' claim is cited without strong source.

**Recommended Cycle 2 Action: MAJOR REWRITE**

Strengthening edits (priority order):
1. **Add direct quotes**: 'PROS-007 predicted: "The global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT in 12 months." Confirmation admitted: "SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025" — the word "alone" tacitly admitting the global field may not meet the threshold.'
2. **Emphasize parameter circularity**: 'The dome lists "Field decay rate: >=28 nT/year" as a core model parameter, not as a derived prediction. Parameters are inputs. Measuring that an observation matches a parameter you configured to match it is circular, not predictive.'
3. **Elevate temporal post-hoc evidence**: Make the 6-day registration-confirmation gap and batch pattern (WIN-035 through WIN-039 all registered 2026-03-06) the opening sentence of detail_evidence.
4. **Replace '~15 nT/yr globally'** with more defensible statement: 'The global dipole moment declines ~5% per century, which translates to surface field intensity decline varying by location — roughly 15-20 nT/yr spatially averaged, but this is far below the 28 nT/yr rate only met in the fastest-declining region (SAA core).'
5. **Strengthen architecture argument**: Add explicit reference to monitor.py Domain 8 monitoring only Tsumeb station, not computing any global average.

**Potential Dome Defense Anticipated**:
- 'You claim the global dipole declines ~15 nT/yr, but there is no single universally-agreed global number. The SAA is scientifically the most important region to monitor.' **Response**: True, but then admit PROS-007's claim was regional, not global. Can't have both — either the SAA is representative (making the prediction less impressive), or it's not (making the prediction misleading by framing it as global).
- 'We derived the 28 nT threshold from our aetheric field model.' **Response**: No derivation appears in the model documentation (raw-text/05-model.txt). The value matches empirical SAA core decay and was adopted from observations. Show the derivation or concede it's circular.

**Verdict Confidence Summary**:
- Regional-Global conflation visible in dome's own text: 95% confidence
- Hardcoded parameter-as-prediction issue: 90% confidence
- Single-station monitoring architecture: 95% confidence (cross-verified with WIN-004/005/035)
- Post-hoc timing pattern: 85% confidence (consistent with batch pattern)
- Overall 'Misleading' verdict: 92% confidence (Cycle 2 revalidation)

**Code_analysis Tags Revalidation: ALL HOLD**
- monitoring='hardcoded' ✓ (threshold comparison against static input)
- relabels_standard=true ✓ (dome calls it 'aetheric decay', standard calls it core convection)
- post_hoc=true ✓ (6-day turnaround with pre-public data)
- derives_from_dome=false ✓ (no derivation from dome geometry; value adopted from observation)
- reviewed=true ✓ (Cycle 1 done; Cycle 2 confirms)

citations_verified: NOAA https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model — WMM data portal. Current text uses generic NOAA link; specific WMM link would be stronger.
Thébault et al., 2015 IGRF-12 (doi: 10.1186/s40623-015-0228-9) — IGRF-12 international standard reference field. Published and verifiable.
raw-text/02-wins.txt lines 44-45 — Direct quotes from dome's V51.0 raw text, verified against actual text.
raw-text/05-model.txt lines 42, 75 — Direct quotes from dome's model documentation, verified.
CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic model — Industry standard for historical field reconstruction (ESA/Swarm), verifiable.
citations_unchecked: '~15 nT/yr as global average' — This depends on spatial averaging method. Finlay et al. (2010) 'International Geomagnetic Reference Field' and NCEI WMM2025 technical reports would support this, but no specific citation in current WIN text. This is Cycle 1's identified weakness. Recommend either citing a specific paper or reframing to avoid this number (use dome's own 'SAA region alone' quote instead).

'20-35 nT/yr SAA core decay' — Plausible from INTERMAGNET Tsumeb (TTB) and Hermanus (HER) stations and WMM technical reports, but current WIN text doesn't cite the specific source. Should cite WMM2025 technical documentation or CHAOS-7 model output.

Monitor.py Domain 8 architecture — Inferred from cross-references in WIN-004/005/035 reviews; source code (monitor.py) not directly available in repository as extracted file. This is noted in Cycle 1 review and remains true in Cycle 2.
